Buildforfun blog posts!

How to problem solve

The system of probleming, i extracted from Polya’s book how to solve it.

  1. U - Understand the problem
  2. P - Plan - Thinking of a plan to solve the problem. Other similar cases, does it go back to any root in science.
  3. C - Carrying out the plan

  4. V - Validate the answer - how to check we are right?

  5. Understanding the problem.
    • Write the problem down clearly and precisely.
    • What is unknown?
    • What are the data?
    • What is the condition?
    • Is it possible to satisfy the condition?
    • Is the condition sufficient to determine the unknown?
    • Or is it insufficient?
    • Or redundant?
    • Or contradictory?
    • Draw a figure. Introudce suitable notation.
    • Separate various parts of the condition. Can you write them down?
  6. Devising a plan
    • Have you seen it before?
    • Have you seen the same problem in a different form?
    • Do you know a theorem that could be useful?
    • Look at the unknown! And try to think of a familar problem or a similar unknown.
    • Should you introduce some auxiliary element in order to make its use possible?
    • Could you restate the problem?
    • Could you restate it still differently?
    • Go back to definitions.
    • Could you solve a part of the problem?
    • Could you derive something useful from the data?
    • Could you think of other data appropriate to determine the unknown?
    • Could you change the unknown or the data, or both if necessary, so that the new unknown and the new data are nearer to each other?
    • Did you use all the data?
    • Have you taken into account all essential notions involved in the problem?
    • You should obtain eventually a plan of the solution. Step away for a while to help mind think.
    • Writing out the final solution clearly.Expressing the solution forced rigorous thinking and exposed any gaps.
  7. Carrying out the plan
    • Carrying out your plan of solution, check each step
    • Can you see clearly that the step is correct?
    • Can you prove that it is correct?
  8. Looking back and validating- checking the answer
    • Can you check the result?
    • Can you check the argument?
    • Can you derive the result differently?
    • Can you see it at a glance?
    • Can you use the result, or the method, for some othre problem?
